questions.question:
For any discrete random variable X, with probability mass function
\(P(X=j)=p_{j}, p_{j}\geq 0, j \in \{0,....N\}\) and \( \sum_{j=0}^{N}p_{j}=1\), define the polynomial function \(g_{x}(z)=\sum_{j=0}^{N}p_{j}z^{j}\). For a certain discrete random variable Y, there exists a scalar \(\beta \in \)[0,1] such that \(g_{Y}(z)=(1-\beta +\beta z)^{N}\). The expectation of Y is

(GATE CSE 2017 SET-2)
options:
D.

Not expressible in terms of N and \(\beta\) alone
